text:
# Deliver Initiatives  
Design, Code and Test  
Pre Sprint  
Ensures that our sprint teams are well-equipped to deliver  
high-quality digital solutions efficiently and predictably.  
Agile “sprint” Teams  
By focusing on data-driven and engineering-driven practices,  ensures optimal resource and time utilization, along with evidence-based quality checks at all levels.  
Post Sprint  
Route to Live  
By focusing on efficient post-sprint processes, we ensure  our solutions are deployed quickly and reliably, minimizing risk and maximizing value for our clients.  
2-week sprints  
Ready for coding  
End of  
coding  
Initiatives  
Dev  
- Reusable code components and snippets
- Digital microservices builder
- Automated build and deployment process
- Trunk based development and feature toggling with Launch Darkly
- Gamification – rewarding individuals who produce high quality code
- GenAI for code simplification, refactoring, unit testing, debugging  
Testing  
- Implementing developer led automated UI testing – align technology
- Test embedded in dev process (review unit tests, approve PR’s, etc)
- GenAI – test scenarios, test scripts from requirements
- Introducing environment virtualisation stubs to remove requirement on physical environments and data (50% reduction of environments)
- Implement technology specific test automation frameworks
- Address test automation debt – 70% automation achieved
- ROI for automation
- Embedded technical leaders (IBM code owners, tech lead, SME’s)  
Initiatives  
- Streamlining post sprint go live signoffs to enable a deployment ever 2 weeks  
Visits to India to remove cultural, organisational and capability divides  
Design boards to bring together design decisions with technical feasibility  
Initiatives  
- Produce screen designs that meet both technical and business requirements.
- Clear Solution Intent to align stakeholders and set expectations.
- Identify dependencies and potential risks to enable accurate work estimation and planning.
- Standardised and reusable patterns
- GenAI Code Analyser
- GenAI for userstories & doc
- Enabling Figma Dev licences to improve visibility, versioning, and tracking  
Over 200 but here are some of the big hitters  
Epic Refinement  
& Prioritisation  
App Support  
Predictability  
Faster & Better Build & Test  
Reliable Releases  
The picture is a complex data visualization titled "Trend of PR Review Count vs Comments (Native)."  
The graph tracks metrics across years, specifically spanning "2023," "2024," and "2025," with monthly labels from "January" through "November." The vertical axis provides numerical counts up to 200.  
A legend defines four key categories:
1. Total No of PRs completed
2. Green (Suggestions & Recommendations)
3. Amber (Good to have)
4. Red (Need to Fix)  
The footer includes corporate branding and confidential information: "nationwide IBM" and the date "21/01/26." The copyright notice reads: "© 2025 - Nationwide Building Society and IBM Confidential," and the page number is "16."  
The image is a slide presentation detailing "Hackathon 2025."  
The slide contains explanatory text and a visual process diagram outlining four phases that lead to ideas getting executed. The text states: "We will have 4 phases that will lead to Ideas getting executed. We are working out on the duration (3 days versus 4-5 weeks)."  
The four phases listed in bullet points are:
1. **Discovery** – Nationwide + IBM Leadership to identify the theme for the teams to ideate.
2. **Ideation** – team to submit ideas for selection for POC.
3. **POC** – Team to demo the selected ideas for selection and award.
4. **Selection** – judges to rank the ideas based on the decided criteria. The highest ranked ideas to get sponsorship for execution.  
The visual flow diagram, which represents the process, proceeds through several steps:  
*   **Discovery:** The icon is a target/dartboard. The text describes this step as: "Identify strategic focus area and problem areas." This phase is allocated "3 weeks."
*   **Ideation:** The icon is a lightbulb. The text describes this step as: "Conceptualize ideas, refine theme, and unique ideas." This phase is allocated "3 weeks."
*   **POC:** The icon is a magnifying glass. The text describes this step as: "Conceptualize ideas, aligned with strategic goal." This phase is allocated "4 weeks."
*   **Selection:** The icon is a trophy. The text describes this step as: "Prioritize and budget ideas, develop proposal." This phase is allocated "2 weeks."
*   **Sponsorship & Execution:** The icon shows a group of people. The text describes this step as: "Add the idea to the digital roadmap for execution."  
Beneath the main process, there is an additional note: "We have a good pipeline of ideas, many of which can be taken up during this hackathon."  
The footer includes the logos and names "nationwide" and "ibm," and the copyright text: "© 2025 - Nationwide Building Society and IBM Confidential." The date listed is "21/01/26," and the slide number is "28."  
The image is a close-up architectural photograph showing the facade of a modern building set against a clear, light sky. The structure is characterized by a highly geometric and repetitive pattern of staggered windows. The facade is clad in two main materials: dark gray, rectangular tiling that covers the majority of the wall surface, and lighter gray, horizontal rectangular cladding that frames the windows. The windows themselves are angled and staggered across the face of the building, creating a strong sense of pattern and depth.  
A week in India – 2 journeys and 1 Hackathon  
Close Accounts  
Goal: Complete the journey for close account (in React) and make it ready for the developers to pick up in Hackathon.  
Order Tax Certificate  
Goal: Design the order certificate journey (in Native) and make it ready for the developers to pick up in Hackathon.  
Hackathon  
Goal: Challenge the development teams to deliver on the 2 journeys in both React and Native to understand which gets delivered at pace and also to understand how the teams understand the designs shared with them and deliver on them. This is also to identify how we can further streamline our design handoff process and nurture the close collaboration between design and development teams.
name: Chunk 5/8

raw_entities.name: Nationwide Building Society
raw_entities.description: A major UK building society and strategic partner engaged in a collaborative digital transformation and agile delivery programme with IBM. The client is actively involved in co-creating solutions through hackathons, sprint planning, and joint leadership initiatives to modernise digital customer journeys and streamline development workflows.
context: © 2025 - Nationwide Building Society and IBM Confidential
We are working out on the duration (3 days versus 4-5 weeks).
Goal: Complete the journey for close account (in React) and make it ready for the developers to pick up in Hackathon.
Goal: Design the order certificate journey (in Native) and make it ready for the developers to pick up in Hackathon.

raw_entities.name: IBM
raw_entities.description: A global technology and consulting firm serving as the primary delivery partner for Nationwide Building Society. IBM provides embedded technical leadership, AI-driven development tools, and agile sprint methodologies to co-deliver digital solutions and drive operational modernisation.
context: © 2025 - Nationwide Building Society and IBM Confidential

Nationwide + IBM Leadership to identify the theme for the teams to ideate.
The image is a slide presentation detailing "Hackathon 2025."

raw_entities.name: Agile Sprint Teams
raw_entities.description: A structured delivery framework utilising data-driven and engineering-driven practices to ensure optimal resource allocation, time efficiency, and evidence-based quality checks. The solution is designed to equip development teams for predictable, high-quality digital delivery across pre-sprint, sprint, and post-sprint phases.
context: Agile “sprint” Teams By focusing on data-driven and engineering-driven practices, ensures optimal resource and time utilization, along with evidence-based quality checks at all levels.
Ensures that our sprint teams are well-equipped to deliver high-quality digital solutions efficiently and predictably.
2-week sprints
Pre Sprint Ensures that our sprint teams are well-equipped to deliver high-quality digital solutions efficiently and predictably.

raw_entities.name: Hackathon 2025
raw_entities.description: A structured innovation and proof-of-concept programme designed to accelerate idea execution through discovery, ideation, POC, and selection phases. The programme serves as a collaborative engine to test delivery approaches, streamline design handoffs, and identify high-value initiatives for the digital roadmap.
context: The image is a slide presentation detailing "Hackathon 2025."
We will have 4 phases that will lead to Ideas getting executed.
Hackathon Goal: Challenge the development teams to deliver on the 2 journeys in both React and Native to understand which gets delivered at pace
We have a good pipeline of ideas, many of which can be taken up during this hackathon.

raw_entities.name: Automated Build and Deployment Process
raw_entities.description: A technical delivery capability that automates the compilation, testing, and release of software components. This capability reduces manual intervention, accelerates release cycles, and ensures consistent deployment standards across development environments.
context: - Automated build and deployment process
Address test automation debt – 70% automation achieved
Implementing developer led automated UI testing – align technology
Implement technology specific test automation frameworks

raw_entities.name: Trunk Based Development and Feature Toggling
raw_entities.description: A modern software engineering practice that maintains a single main code branch and uses feature flags to control code visibility. This capability enables safer, faster releases and reduces merge conflicts while allowing controlled rollout of new functionality.
context: - Trunk based development and feature toggling with Launch Darkly
Pre Sprint Ensures that our sprint teams are well-equipped to deliver high-quality digital solutions efficiently and predictably.

raw_entities.name: GenAI Code Simplification and Refactoring
raw_entities.description: An artificial intelligence-driven capability that assists developers in simplifying complex code, refactoring legacy structures, and generating unit tests. This capability accelerates development velocity, improves code quality, and reduces debugging time through automated AI assistance.
context: - GenAI for code simplification, refactoring, unit testing, debugging

raw_entities.description: The digital transformation of the customer account closure process, leveraging React-based development and hackathon-driven validation. context: Address test automation debt – 70% automation achieved
Implementing developer led automated UI testing – align technology
Implement technology specific test automation frameworks
ROI for automation

entities.name: Physical Environment and Data Dependencies
entities.description: An operational constraint where development and testing workflows are bottlenecked by the need for physical environments and real data. This challenge increases provisioning time, raises infrastructure costs, and limits testing agility until virtualisation is implemented.
context: Introducing environment virtualisation stubs to remove requirement on physical environments and data (50% reduction of environments)
remove requirement on physical environments and data
Identify dependencies and potential risks to enable accurate work estimation and planning.
Standardised and reusable patterns

entities.name: Launch Darkly
entities.description: A feature flagging and release management platform integrated into the development workflow. This technology enables trunk-based development by allowing teams to merge code safely and toggle features on or off without redeploying, supporting faster and safer releases.
context: - Trunk based development and feature toggling with Launch Darkly
Trunk based development and feature toggling
Pre Sprint Ensures that our sprint teams are well-equipped to deliver high-quality digital solutions efficiently and predictably.
2-week sprints

entities.name: React
entities.description: A JavaScript UI library used for building the close account journey interface. This technology enables component-based development, improves rendering performance, and supports rapid prototyping during hackathon-driven development cycles.
context: Goal: Complete the journey for close account (in React) and make it ready for the developers to pick up in Hackathon.
Hackathon Goal: Challenge the development teams to deliver on the 2 journeys in both React and Native to understand which gets delivered at pace
in React
Produce screen designs that meet both technical and business requirements.

entities.name: Native
entities.description: A platform-specific development approach used for building the order certificate journey. This technology leverages device-native capabilities to ensure optimal performance, security, and user experience for mobile or desktop applications.
context: Goal: Design the order certificate journey (in Native) and make it ready for the developers to pick up in Hackathon.
Hackathon Goal: Challenge the development teams to deliver on the 2 journeys in both React and Native to understand which gets delivered at pace
in Native
The picture is a complex data visualization titled "Trend of PR Review Count vs Comments (Native)."

entities.name: Figma
entities.description: A cloud-based design and prototyping platform enabled through developer licences. This technology improves design visibility, version control, and tracking, bridging the gap between design and development teams to streamline handoffs and reduce rework.
context: - Enabling Figma Dev licences to improve visibility, versioning, and tracking
Enabling Figma Dev licences to improve visibility, versioning, and tracking
Design boards to bring together design decisions with technical feasibility
This is also to identify how we can further streamline our design handoff process and nurture the close collaboration between design and development teams.

entities.name: Gamified Quality Incentives
entities.description: A unique delivery differentiator that uses gamification to reward developers for producing high-quality code. This approach shifts quality from a compliance requirement to a motivated behaviour, driving higher engagement and reducing defect rates organically.
context: - Gamification – rewarding individuals who produce high quality code
Gamification – rewarding individuals who produce high quality code
evidence-based quality checks at all levels
Address test automation debt – 70% automation achieved

entities.name: Embedded Technical Leadership
entities.description: A competitive advantage where senior technical leaders, code owners, and subject matter experts are embedded directly within client delivery teams. This ensures real-time architectural guidance, faster decision-making, and tighter alignment between business goals and technical execution.
context: - Embedded technical leaders (IBM code owners, tech lead, SME’s)
Embedded technical leaders (IBM code owners, tech lead, SME’s)
Identify dependencies and potential risks to enable accurate work estimation and planning.
Standardised and reusable patterns

entities.name: Bi-Weekly Deployment Cadence
entities.description: A delivery differentiator that enables consistent bi-weekly production deployments through streamlined post-sprint sign-off processes. This capability reduces release bottlenecks, accelerates time-to-market, and provides clients with predictable, frequent value delivery.
context: Streamlining post sprint go live signoffs to enable a deployment ever 2 weeks
Streamlining post sprint go live signoffs to enable a deployment ever 2 weeks
2-week sprints
By focusing on efficient post-sprint processes, we ensure our solutions are deployed quickly and reliably

entities.name: Strong Innovation Pipeline
entities.description: A competitive advantage characterised by a robust and continuous flow of validated ideas ready for execution. This pipeline ensures sustained innovation, reduces ideation-to-delivery latency, and provides a steady stream of high-impact initiatives for the digital roadmap.
context: We have a good pipeline of ideas, many of which can be taken up during this hackathon.
good pipeline of ideas, many of which can be taken up during this hackathon.
The picture is a complex data visualization titled "Trend of PR Review Count vs Comments (Native)."
The image is a slide presentation detailing "Hackathon 2025."

entities.name: India
entities.description: A key geographic delivery location hosting development and testing teams. This location is strategically leveraged to bridge cultural and organisational divides, enable cross-site collaboration, and support the execution of digital journeys and hackathon initiatives.
context: Visits to India to remove cultural, organisational and capability divides
A week in India – 2 journeys and 1 Hackathon
Visits to India to remove cultural, organisational and capability divides
The image is a slide presentation detailing "Hackathon 2025."

entities.name: Hackathon 2025
entities.description: A structured innovation event designed to accelerate idea execution through discovery, ideation, POC, and selection phases. This event serves as a collaborative proving ground to test delivery approaches, validate digital journeys, and identify high-value initiatives for sponsorship and roadmap integration.
context: The image is a slide presentation detailing "Hackathon 2025."
We will have 4 phases that will lead to Ideas getting executed.
Hackathon Goal: Challenge the development teams to deliver on the 2 journeys in both React and Native to understand which gets delivered at pace
We have a good pipeline of ideas, many of which can be taken up during this hackathon.
